eventTitle: Semiconductor ETF headline cluster with liquidity and drawdown checks
researchQuestion: Use retained ETF event triage, liquidity regime, catalyst follow-up, and portfolio risk gates for a research-only ETF event review.

freshInputs.name: public headlines
freshInputs.summary: Multiple public headlines mention semiconductor supply-chain pressure and ETF concentration risk.

freshInputs.name: ETF issuer notes
freshInputs.summary: Issuer notes describe top holdings, sector concentration, and index methodology exposure.

freshInputs.name: earnings calendar
freshInputs.summary: Upcoming top-holding earnings dates are listed for catalyst follow-up only.

freshInputs.name: company IR notes
freshInputs.summary: Company IR pages provide event source context without adding a price forecast.

freshInputs.name: credit spreads
freshInputs.summary: Credit spread proxy is stable enough that the event is not treated as broad credit stress.

freshInputs.name: funding stress indicators
freshInputs.summary: Funding stress proxy is monitored as a liquidity-regime gate rather than a trading trigger.

freshInputs.name: drawdown context
freshInputs.summary: Recent drawdown context is checked before any qualitative risk implication.

freshInputs.name: volatility indicators
freshInputs.summary: Volatility proxy is elevated enough to require wait discipline and red-team invalidation.

freshInputs.name: current position weights
freshInputs.summary: Current weights are available for checking whether the ETF event creates concentration risk.

freshInputs.name: recent price history
freshInputs.summary: Recent price history is available for volatility, drawdown, and sample-window checks.

freshInputs.name: realized volatility estimate
freshInputs.summary: Realized volatility estimate is available for separating elevated risk from narrative conviction.

freshInputs.name: drawdown history
freshInputs.summary: Drawdown history is available for checking whether the move is normal noise or regime stress.

freshInputs.name: correlation matrix
freshInputs.summary: Correlation matrix is available for checking whether the holding is moving with broader risk exposures.

freshInputs.name: ETF or index exposure map
freshInputs.summary: Exposure map is available for linking the ETF event to major sector, factor, or index exposures.

freshInputs.name: macro rate context
freshInputs.summary: Macro rate context is available for checking whether valuation or duration pressure amplifies the event.

freshInputs.name: factor definition
freshInputs.summary: The timing factor is defined before any validation claim is made.

freshInputs.name: ETF or asset universe
freshInputs.summary: The ETF or asset universe is frozen for sample-out and benchmark checks.

freshInputs.name: rebalance schedule
freshInputs.summary: The rebalance schedule is available for detecting timing leakage and turnover pressure.

freshInputs.name: walk-forward split
freshInputs.summary: Walk-forward split is available so the timing signal is not judged only in-sample.

freshInputs.name: out-of-sample returns
freshInputs.summary: Out-of-sample returns are available for checking whether factor timing survives beyond the fit window.

freshInputs.name: benchmark returns
freshInputs.summary: Benchmark returns are available for comparing the timing signal to a simpler baseline.

freshInputs.name: transaction cost assumption
freshInputs.summary: Transaction cost assumption is available for testing whether turnover destroys the apparent edge.

freshInputs.name: turnover estimate
freshInputs.summary: Turnover estimate is available for checking whether factor timing is too costly or fragile.

freshInputs.name: whipsaw log
freshInputs.summary: Whipsaw log is available for identifying false timing switches and noisy regime transitions.

freshInputs.name: confounder notes
freshInputs.summary: Confounder notes are available for checking whether the factor is just rates, sector, beta, or liquidity exposure in disguise.

redTeamInvalidation: If issuer exposure is small or unrelated to the headline cluster, the ETF event frame is weak.
If credit and funding stress remain benign, do not promote the headline cluster into a liquidity-regime thesis.
If volatility rises while source evidence stays thin, keep the output at watch-only research framing.
